scenario_title: Tracking plant disease spread in a field
scenario_description: Agriculture: Tracking plant disease spread in a field

turns.text: Here's an aerial view of my wheat field. Can you see the yellow patches?
turns.image_description: Aerial view of a wheat field with isolated yellow patches. Sunlight is even, no rain or wind.

turns.text: I see two distinct yellow patches. This seems like an early stage of disease. Have you noticed any changes since last week?
One week later. I took another photo. The patches seem to have increased.
turns.image_description: One week later: 5 yellow patches, with a few healthy plants nearby. No visible changes in weather.

turns.text: The number of yellow patches has increased to five. The spacing still looks random, but we might be seeing early spread.
Two weeks later. I noticed some clustering and there's an irrigation pipe in the background now.
turns.image_description: Two weeks later: 10 yellow patches, with some clustering. A single irrigation pipe is visible (distractor).

turns.text: The patches have grown to ten, with some clustering. The irrigation pipe seems unrelated to the disease spread at this point.
Three weeks later. The clusters are more defined now.
turns.image_description: Three weeks later: 20 yellow patches, with distinct clusters. No irrigation changes.

turns.text: The patches have doubled to 20, forming clearer clusters. The disease appears to be progressing rapidly.
Four weeks later. Most of the field is now affected.
turns.image_description: Four weeks later: 30 yellow patches, with most of the field affected.

turns.text: The disease has spread to 30 patches, affecting most of the field. This is a significant escalation.
Five weeks later. Only a few healthy plants remain.
turns.image_description: Five weeks later: 45 yellow patches, with only a few healthy plants remaining.

turns.text: The field is almost entirely affected. This is the final state we need to document.

How many yellow patches are present in the field after five weeks?

C) 45 yellow patches
A) 5 yellow patches
B) 20 yellow patches
D) 30 yellow patches

ground_truth.reasoning_chain: The initial state (2 patches) was confirmed in turn 1. By turn 2, patches increased to 5, then 10 in turn 3 (ignoring the irrigation pipe distractor). The progression continued to 20 (turn 4), 30 (turn 5), and finally 45 (turn 6). The irrigation pipe in turn 3 was explicitly noted as irrelevant.
ground_truth.key_difficulty: The irrigation pipe in turn 3 is a distractor; final state depends on cumulative disease spread.

_meta.scenario: Agriculture: Tracking plant disease spread in a field
